title: Expert review of verification methodology (guard-digit analysis, BBP spot-check sufficiency, two-method independence)
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. A computational-mathematics / numerical-analysis SME reviews and signs off the verification methodology; this medium-risk sign-off is a prerequisite for any record-scale publication (M4) and must be recorded independently of whoever ran the computation.
objective: Obtain and record an SME review and sign-off of the verification methodology (guard-digit derivation, BBP spot-check sufficiency, two-method independence).
acceptanceCriteria: A computational-mathematics / numerical-analysis SME reviews and signs off the guard-digit derivation, the BBP spot-check count vs. its stated probability bound, and the genuineness of backend/two-method independence; sign-off recorded.
This sign-off is a prerequisite for any record-scale publication (M4).
output: A document recording the SME methodology review and sign-off gating record-scale publication.
